description: In an apartment living room, Chandler, Joey, and Monica sit on a sofa facing a television out of view. Rachel sits on the floor leaning against a coffee table, and Phoebe sits in an armchair. All face the same direction.
A woman's voice from the off-screen television says, "It's not fair."
Rachel brings a piece of popcorn to her mouth and chews.

A man's voice on the television says, "It's my money." A woman's voice responds, "I know, but it's what Brad wanted." Rachel turns toward Phoebe, points toward the television, and asks, "Okay, which one is, uh," while Phoebe answers, "that's Brad's widow."
Rachel continues, "Now, why is she so"
Rachel finishes, "upset?" Phoebe replies, "Because she just found out she was cut out of the" and Rachel interjects, "Ugh."
Rachel gestures with her hands and says, "Doesn't she know you can't define yourself in terms of money?"
Rachel continues, "That it's about values and morals and your ability to give and receive love?"
Phoebe maintains her gaze toward Rachel.
Phoebe replies, "No," her facial expression remaining neutral.
The view changes to the television screen, displaying a man in a white medical coat hugging a woman in a black dress.
On the television, the man says, "I'm sorry, Amber. It's just like Brad to have to have the last word." The group continues watching from the sofa.
The front door opens behind the sofa.
Ross enters rapidly, closing the door behind him. "I'm sorry I'm late. What happened?" he asks. Joey raises a hand to shush him, and Monica says, "We just want to see the end."
On the television, Amber says, "I want you, Drake." Drake replies, "I know you do."
On the television, Drake continues, "But you and I can never be together that way."
Amber asks, "What?"
Drake replies, "There's something I never told you, Amber. I'm actually your half brother."
In the living room, Chandler, Joey, and Monica gasp loudly, bringing their hands to their mouths. Ross stands behind the sofa with his mouth open.
Phoebe leans forward and asks Joey, "So what happens next?"
Joey says, "Well, I get the medical award for separating the Siamese twins." He holds up a finger as he speaks.
Joey gestures with his hands and adds, "Then Amber and I go to Venezuela to meet our other half brother"
Joey finishes, "Ramon."
Joey continues looking at Monica, keeping his hands raised.
Joey moves his hands far apart and says, "And that's where I find the world's biggest emerald."
Joey nods slightly and adds, "It's really big, but it's cursed."
Chandler, Monica, and Ross stare at Joey. Phoebe smiles widely, and Rachel laughs.
Chandler, keeping his arms crossed, says, "God, that is good"
Chandler smiles briefly.
Joey laughs. The screen turns black.
The exterior of a brick corner building is shown during the day, with a dark awning and a white car parked on the street.
Now inside an apartment, Chandler stands at a foosball table. Phoebe stands nearby with her arms crossed. Chandler says, "TVs. Play"
Chandler continues, "with me." Phoebe shakes her head and says, "No."
Phoebe keeps her arms crossed.
Phoebe gestures toward the table and says, "This game is grotesque. 20 armless guys joined at the waist by a steel bar,"
Phoebe lowers her arms.
Phoebe waves her hands as she speaks, "forced to play soccer forever."
Phoebe crosses her arms again.
Phoebe says, "Low human rights violation."
Chandler lowers his head slightly.
Chandler raises his hands and says, "Thieves don't feel so bad for me."
Chandler steps back, adding, "After they're done playing, I break out the little plastic women and everybody has a pretty good time."
Chandler lowers his hands and looks at Phoebe, who looks back with a slight frown.
Phoebe walks past Chandler toward the refrigerator. She asks, "Why don't you play with your roommate?"
Chandler picks up a small yellow ball from the counter and tosses it in his hand.
Chandler catches the ball and says, "He's not a big fan of foosball."
Phoebe opens a bottle of beer at the counter. "Uhoh, are we not getting along with the new boy?" she asks. Chandler says, "No, he's all right."
Chandler keeps his gaze on Phoebe.
Chandler says, "He spends most of his time in his room." Phoebe points her bottle toward him and says, "Maybe that's because you haven't taken the time to get to know him. Let's remedy"
Chandler and Phoebe look at each other.
Phoebe finishes, "that, shall we?" Chandler shakes his head, saying, "We don't need to remedy that." Phoebe walks toward the door, saying, "Oh, yeah. It'll be fun."
Chandler throws the small yellow ball toward the door. The door opens, and a man enters.
Chandler asks, "What was that?" The man says, "Hi."
The man stands near the open doorway.

Phoebe gestures with her free hand and says to the man, "I just thought that it would be fun if the three of us had some beers and got to know each other." The man replies, "All right. That sounds all right."
Phoebe smiles and says, "Oh, good. Okay."
Phoebe suddenly turns away and places a hand on Chandler's chest, saying, "Oh, no, I have to go because I'm late for my"
Phoebe moves toward the door, continuing, "Green Eggs and Ham discussion group"
Chandler and the man watch Phoebe step toward the doorway.
Phoebe pauses at the doorway and says, "tonight. It's why he would not eat them on a train."
Chandler and the man stand in silence, looking at Phoebe.
Chandler says, "Have fun."
Phoebe waves and says, "Bye." Chandler whispers to Phoebe, "That was so lame."
Phoebe whispers back, "I know." Chandler whispers, "Yeah."
Phoebe stays near the doorway.
Phoebe whispers, "Okay. Talk to him," then steps out into the hallway and closes the door.
Chandler stands near the kitchen counter and slowly turns his head toward the man, who remains stationary by the door.
Chandler says, "So"
Chandler shifts his weight slightly.
Chandler continues, "you think that Speed Racer guy gets a lot of tickets."
The man stares at Chandler without moving or responding.
In an apartment living room, Eddie sits on a sofa holding a beer bottle, while Chandler sits in a chair across from him. Eddie smiles and gestures with his free hand.

Eddie points and says, "That's good. So who broke up with who?" Chandler, holding a beer bottle, gestures with one hand and replies, "What, are you kidding? I broke up with her. She actually thought that Sean Penn was the capital of"
Chandler drops his hand and finishes, "Cambodia."
Eddie laughs and points his finger.
Eddie rubs his eye and says, "That's good, man, when everybody knows that the capital of Cambodia is" pausing with his hand near his face.
Both men sit facing each other in the living room.
Eddie lowers his bottle and says, "well, it's not Sean Penn."
Eddie sits forward on the sofa.
Eddie leans forward, gesturing with both hands, and says, "All right? I got a funny one, all right?"
Eddie continues, "My last girlfriend Tilly, okay, we're eating breakfast, right? And I made all these"
Eddie continues, "pancakes. There was, like, 50 pancakes, right? And all of a sudden, she turns to me, all right, and she says, Eddie I said, yeah? She says," as he points toward Chandler.
Eddie says, "Eddie, I don't want to see you anymore."
He mimes reaching into his chest and pulling something out with his hand, adding, "And it was literally like she had reached into my chest, ripped out my heart"
Eddie makes broad smearing motions in the air, saying, "and smeared it all over my life."
Chandler watches Eddie silently.
Eddie gestures downward with both hands, saying, "It was like this incredible abyss and I'm falling and I keep falling..."
Eddie continues, "...and I don't think I'm ever going to stop," before throwing his head back and laughing loudly.
Chandler stares forward silently. Eddie runs a hand through his hair, his laughter subsiding into a sigh, and he rests his elbow on his knee.
Eddie looks forward, his posture slumped, and says, "that wasn't such a funny story, was it?"
Chandler maintains a fixed gaze on Eddie.
The setting changes to a coffee shop at night. Inside, Phoebe stands playing an acoustic guitar and singing into a microphone.
Phoebe sings, "And a crusty old man said I'll do what I can..." strumming her guitar.
Phoebe continues singing, "...and the rest of the rats played miraculous." Richard, Monica, Rachel, Ross, Joey, and Chandler sit on surrounding sofas listening.
The group on the sofas and other patrons in the coffee shop applaud.
Phoebe waves and says, "that's it. Thanks. Good night."
Richard leans close to Monica over the back of the sofa.
Richard says to Monica, "Phoebe's got another job. Right,"
Phoebe steps down from the stage area.
Monica nods and replies, "Right."
Phoebe sits in an armchair. Joey says, "That's an," and Phoebe replies, "I know."
Ross turns his head toward Rachel.
Ross says, "Well, we should probably get going." Richard adds, "We should go too. I got patience at eight in the morning."
Monica turns to Richard and says, "You know, I was"
Monica continues, "thinking. You know how we always stay at your apartment? Well, I thought maybe tonight we'd stay at my place."
Richard looks at Monica and replies, "I don't know."
Richard continues, "I don't have my jammies."
Monica smiles at Richard.
Monica smiles and says softly, "Well, maybe you don't need them."
Ross turns away from them, raising one hand, and says, "My baby sister, ladies and gentlemen," his volume rising.
Richard laughs and touches Monica's shoulder, and she smiles.
Monica looks toward Ross and says, "shut up. I'm happy."
The group begins to gather their things and stand up.
Phoebe claps her hands together and says, "Oh, this is so nice. All right, I have to make a speech."
Phoebe continues, "I just want to say that of all the guys that Monica has been with and that"
Phoebe tilts her head and adds, "is a lot,"
Richard looks down at Monica, who presses her lips together.
Phoebe looks at Richard and says, "I like you the best."
Richard smiles and replies, "Oh, thank you, Phoebes."
Richard adds, "That's very sweet." Monica stands up and says, "Okay."
Richard holds Monica's jacket as she slides her arms in, and he says, "Hear that? She likes me best."
Richard continues, "And apparently there have been a lot," looking at Monica.
Monica turns to face Richard.
Monica says, "Not a lot," her lips pressed together in a smile.
Monica shakes her head slightly.
Monica adds, "Phoebe's kidding."
Monica laughs shortly and says, "Phoebe's crazy."
Richard begins putting on his own jacket as Ross walks past them.
Ross looks back as he walks and says, "Phoebe's dead."
Phoebe's eyes widen as she stands holding her guitar.
The setting changes to an exterior view of a brick apartment building at night.
Inside an apartment, Chandler opens the front door to reveal a woman standing in the hallway holding a fish tank.
The woman smiles and says, "Hi, I'm looking for Eddie Minowic."
Chandler looks at her and points a thumb over his shoulder.
Chandler says, "Oh, he's not here right now. I'm Chandler. Can I take a message or a fish tank?" gesturing with his hands.
The woman says, "Thanks," and steps inside. Chandler steps back, gesturing inward, and says, "Come on in." The woman adds, "I'm Tilly."
Chandler takes the fish tank from Tilly and carries it to the kitchen counter, placing it down carefully.
Chandler stands up straight, turns back toward Tilly, and says, "Oh."
Tilly points a finger at Chandler and says, "I gather by that o that he told you about me."
Chandler turns away slightly and says, "Oh, yeah. Your name came up in a conversation..."
Chandler looks back at her and continues, "...that terrified me to my very soul," his expression remaining neutral.
Tilly presses her lips together and looks down briefly.
Tilly looks back at Chandler and says, "He's kind of, huh?"
Chandler gestures with his hand and says, "Yes. Hey, can I ask you, is Eddie a little" and Tilly asks, "a little what?"
Chandler gestures again and asks, "A bit country?"
Tilly's brows furrow. Behind her, Eddie appears in the open doorway holding a paper grocery bag.
In an apartment kitchen, Chandler stands near the counter. Eddie stands in the open doorway holding a paper grocery bag, with Tilly standing beside him.
Chandler says, "Or".
Chandler slightly shifts his head.
Chandler extends his left arm with his hand open and says, "a little rock and roll?"
Eddie walks fully into the apartment. Tilly turns to look at him.
Chandler drops his arm and says, "Come on in here. Your roomie."
Eddie looks toward Tilly and says, "Hello, Tilly." Tilly replies, "Eddie."

Tilly says, "I just came by to drop off your tank." Eddie walks past her, placing his paper bag on a counter. The bag rustles. He looks down and says, "It was very thoughtful of you." He then turns his head toward her and adds, "It's very thoughtful," as audience laughter is heard.

Eddie stands near the stove with Chandler leaning against the counter beside him. Eddie stares continuously toward Tilly, his jaw tight. Chandler glances between Eddie and Tilly. Audience laughter builds over the silence.
Tilly looks toward Eddie and says, "Well, okay then."
Tilly turns her head toward the open doorway.
Tilly says, "I'm going to go."
Tilly steps out into the hallway. Chandler raises a hand and says, "Bye bye."
Eddie swings his arm up, stepping forward as he loudly says, "Bye bye," then closes the door firmly. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler turns toward the counter, smiling slightly.

Chandler turns back to Eddie and asks, "So we getting a fish?" Eddie suddenly throws both arms out and says, "You went sex with her, didn't you?" Chandler's eyes widen and his mouth falls open. Audience laughter plays.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Joey rushes toward Phoebe, who sits on a couch holding a small item. He carries a magazine.
Joey hands the magazine to Phoebe, saying rapidly, "Check it out, check it out, check it out, check it out."
Phoebe takes the magazine, looking at the cover. "Oh, Soap Opera Digest," she says. She looks up at Joey, adding, "Oh, that's one of my favorite digests."
Joey leans in, pointing at the magazine in Phoebe's hands.
Joey says, "Page 42."
Phoebe repeats, "Page 42," as she flips through the pages.
Joey sits on the armrest of the couch next to Phoebe.
Reading from the magazine, Phoebe says, "Hey. New doc on the block. Days of our lives."
Phoebe continues reading, "Joey Trubiani." She looks at the page and says, "Cool picture," as the audience laughs lightly.
Joey leans over to look at the page, smiling. "Yeah, I look good," he says.
Phoebe continues looking at the magazine while Joey watches her.
Phoebe looks up at Joey and asks, "Hey, is this true that you write a lot of your own lines?"
Joey nods, briefly raising one hand, and replies, "Well, kind of, yeah." Audience laughter sounds.
Joey lowers his hand.
Gesturing with his hand, Joey says, "Like, remember last week when Alex was in the accident? Well, the line in the script was if we don't get this woman to a"

Joey continues, "hospital, she's going to die." He points to his chest. "But I made it, if this woman doesn't get to a hospital, she's not going to live." Phoebe watches him. Audience laughter follows.
Phoebe nods slowly, a slight smile on her face.
Phoebe gestures with an open hand and says, "Okay, I see what you did there."
Joey smiles and nods. Phoebe looks back down at the magazine.
Looking up from the magazine, Phoebe asks, "Aren't you afraid, though that the writers are going to be kind of mad when they read this?"
Joey purses his lips, his expression neutral.
Joey shakes his head slightly and says, "Never really thought about the writers."
Shrugging slightly, Joey says, "The scripts just kind of come to my house." Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe looks back down at the magazine as Joey raises both index fingers.
Joey says, "But you know what?"

Joey taps his chest, saying, "This makes me look good." He gestures forward, "which makes the show look good," and again, "which makes the writers look good." He opens his hands. "So how could they be mad about that?" Audience laughter plays.
The scene changes to a television screen displaying an hourglass with the text "Days of our Lives". The setting then changes to an office.
A woman sitting at a computer desk reads from a magazine and says, "Makes up most of his lines." Audience laughter is heard. She turns back to her monitor and adds, "Son of a"
The woman types on the keyboard. Script text is visible on her computer monitor.
The woman continues typing and says, "write this, jerkweed." The scene changes to an apartment, where Joey stands holding an open script. A messenger with a shoulder bag stands near the door.

Joey looks at the script and reads, "I fall down an elevator shaft." Gesturing with his hands, he looks at the messenger and asks, "What the hell does this mean? I fall down an elevator shaft?" Audience laughter sounds.
The messenger shakes his head slightly.
The messenger says, "I don't know. I just bring the scripts."
Joey looks down at the script, then back at the messenger.
Pointing to his chest, Joey says, "They can't kill me. I'm Francesca's long lost son." The audience laughs.
The messenger lowers his gaze slightly.
The messenger holds out a clipboard and says, "Right."
The messenger extends the clipboard further toward Joey.

The messenger asks, "Could you sign this?" Joey shakes his head, stepping back, and says, "No way. I'm not signing that." The messenger lowers the clipboard, saying, "I don't think that's going to affect the plot of the show," followed by audience laughter.
Joey flips a page in the script.
Joey looks at the script and asks, "How can they do this to me?"
Joey turns and walks over to a red couch, sitting down heavily while keeping his gaze on the script. The messenger watches him.
The messenger turns toward the door and says, "All right, I'm just going to go."
The messenger grasps the doorknob and opens the door.
As he walks out, the messenger says, "Sorry."
The messenger closes the door behind him. Joey remains seated on the couch, his gaze fixed forward.
The scene changes to an apartment. Monica stands near the kitchen table, while Richard stands by the door putting on a jacket.
Monica walks toward Richard, saying, "Well, it wasn't that many guys. I mean, if you consider how many guys there actually are,"

Monica adds, "it's a very small percentage." Richard adjusts his jacket and says, "Not that big a deal. I was just curious." Rachel and Ross walk in through the door past Richard. Rachel says, "Good night, Richard."
Walking past, Ross says, "Good luck, Mon."
The door closes behind Ross and Rachel. Monica and Richard face each other.
Monica claps her hands together, holding them near her chest. "All right, before I tell you, why don't you tell me how many women you've been?" she asks.
Richard raises his eyebrows, glancing upward briefly before looking back at Monica.
Richard says, "Two," and light audience laughter follows.
Monica leans her head forward, her mouth opening and her eyes wide. She continues looking at him. Audience laughter builds.
Monica asks, "How is that possible? I mean, have you seen you?" gesturing toward Richard with her hands. The audience laughs.
Richard shrugs slightly with his hands in his pockets and says, "Well, I mean, what can I say? I was married to Barbara for 30 years. She was my high school sweetheart."
Richard gestures forward and adds, "Now you that's two."
Monica smiles, her arms crossed.
Monica says, "Two it is," accompanied by audience laughter.
Monica walks toward a table and picks up a black jacket.

Holding the jacket, Monica says, "Okay, time for bed. I'm going to go brush my teeth," and walks past Richard toward a hallway. Richard steps forward, saying, "Whoa, whoa, wait a minute. Now," and follows her.
Richard stops outside a closed purple door. He says, "Come on, it's your turn," as audience laughter is heard. He leans against the doorframe, crosses his arms, and adds, "Oh, come."
Richard remains leaning against the doorframe, looking at the closed door.
Richard says, "know, I don't need the actual number. Just a ballpark."
The door opens, and Monica steps out holding a toothbrush.
Monica gestures with her free hand and says, "Okay, it is definitely less than a ballpark."
The setting changes to a bedroom. Rachel sits on the bed, applying nail polish. Ross walks in, approaches the bed, and kneels behind her.
Rachel continues applying nail polish and says, "Wow, I'm so glad I'm not Monica right now."
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "Tell me about it."
Ross leans closer to her and asks, "So what's your magic number?"
Rachel stops painting her nails and turns her head toward Ross with a smile.
Rachel says, "Come on, you know everyone I've been with." Ross leans back slightly and asks, "All both of them?" Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel raises a hand toward Ross.
Rachel points at Ross and says, "Well, there's you," before Ross points back, saying, "better not be doing these in order."
Rachel lowers her hand and places it on Ross's chest.
Rachel says, "Okay."
Rachel keeps her hand on his chest.
Rachel says, "Billy Dreskin."
Ross nods slightly.
Rachel says, "Pete Carney."
Rachel continues, "Barry and oh, Paolo."
Ross raises his eyebrows, leans back, and says, "Oh, yes, the weenie from Tarini."
Rachel wraps her arms around Ross's neck and pulls him closer.
Rachel asks, "Honey, are you jealous? Of Paolo?"
Rachel pulls him closer and says, "Oh, come on. I'm so much happier with you than I ever was with"
Rachel finishes, "him." Ross asks, "Really?" Rachel strokes his face and responds, "Oh, please. That Paolo thing was barely a relationship."
Rachel holds Ross's face with one hand.
Rachel says, "All it really was was know, meaningless, animal sex."
Ross's mouth drops open and his gaze lowers. Rachel pulls back slightly, her hand still on his face, looking at him.
Rachel removes her hand and says, "Okay, you know, that sounded so much better in my head."
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Chandler walks in rapidly, gesturing with both hands, as Eddie turns away from the door.
Chandler says, "Eddie, I didn't sleep with your ex girlfriend." Eddie turns back to Chandler, gesturing, and says, "That's very interesting, you know, because that's exactly what someone who slept"
Eddie finishes, "with her would say."
Chandler lowers his arms, looking at Eddie.
Chandler steps closer, gesturing with his hands, and says, "This is nuts. This is crazy. She came over for, like, two minutes, dropped off a fish tank and left. End of story."
Eddie stares at Chandler.
Eddie asks, "Where's Buddy?"
Chandler asks, "Buddy?"
Eddie gestures toward the fish tank and says, "My fish buddy."
Chandler looks toward the tank, then back to Eddie.
Chandler points toward the tank and says, "There was no fish when she dropped it off." Eddie steps closer, raising his hands, and says, "This is unbelievable."
Eddie shakes his hands in the air.
Eddie points at Chandler and says, "First person, you sleep with my ex girlfriend. Then you insult my intelligence by lying about it. And then you kill my fish? My"
Eddie finishes, "Buddy." Chandler steps forward, gesturing with both hands, and says, "I didn't kill your fish. Look, Eddie"
Chandler places both hands on Eddie's shoulders. Eddie stares at him.
Chandler says, "would you look at what I'm doing here? Now, that can't be smart."
Chandler removes his hands from Eddie's shoulders and walks past him out of view. Eddie remains standing in place, looking straight ahead.
Eddie keeps his mouth closed and nods slowly, then turns his head away, lowering his gaze.
